raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The paper makes a specific contribution claim — adaptive role-conditioned debate as successor to RadCollab-base — and checking whether the thesis, gap statement, and claimed contribution are internally coherent is central to any paper review.","R1.2":"Explicit causal inference claims are present (debate dynamic, not scale, drives the F1 gains; ablation supports this); the premise-to-conclusion chain must be checked for validity.","R1.3":"Concrete numeric claims appear throughout (CheXbert F1 on three benchmarks, 1.5-point average ablation drop, 60-transcript analysis); misalignment between stated figures and what the cited experiments actually support would directly invalidate the results section, making this load-bearing.","R1.4":"The paper explicitly positions RadCollab as a meaningful advance over RadCollab-base and prior single-agent work; assessing whether the delta constitutes genuine novelty rather than incremental tuning is necessary to evaluate the contribution claim.","R1.7":"The artifact cites '(Anonymous Authors, 2024)' for RadCollab-base — a self-citation to a paper that appears to still be under anonymous review — raising attribution, double-submission, and scholarship integrity concerns that must be surfaced regardless of other review scope."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Do you want a full critical review covering logic, evidence alignment, novelty, and citation integrity — or a writing and prose quality check only?"}

rationale.R1.1: The paper makes a specific contribution claim — adaptive role-conditioned debate as successor to RadCollab-base — and checking whether the thesis, gap statement, and claimed contribution are internally coherent is central to any paper review.
rationale.R1.2: Explicit causal inference claims are present (debate dynamic, not scale, drives the F1 gains; ablation supports this); the premise-to-conclusion chain must be checked for validity.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete numeric claims appear throughout (CheXbert F1 on three benchmarks, 1.5-point average ablation drop, 60-transcript analysis); misalignment between stated figures and what the cited experiments actually support would directly invalidate the results section, making this load-bearing.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly positions RadCollab as a meaningful advance over RadCollab-base and prior single-agent work; assessing whether the delta constitutes genuine novelty rather than incremental tuning is necessary to evaluate the contribution claim.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact cites '(Anonymous Authors, 2024)' for RadCollab-base — a self-citation to a paper that appears to still be under anonymous review — raising attribution, double-submission, and scholarship integrity concerns that must be surfaced regardless of other review scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want a full critical review covering logic, evidence alignment, novelty, and citation integrity — or a writing and prose quality check only?
